name: Orekit
description: Free, open-source Java library for space dynamics. Provides high-fidelity orbit propagation, attitude determination, and event detection. Used by ESA and commercial satellite operators for mission planning and operations.
whyUseIt: Orekit is the open-source alternative to STK for operational astrodynamics. It handles all the complexity — atmospheric drag models, solar radiation pressure, third-body perturbations, and Earth gravity harmonics — with validated, flight-proven algorithms.

vocabulary.definition: Two-Line Element — a compact format for describing satellite orbits, published by the US Space Force
Forces that cause an orbit to deviate from a perfect Keplerian ellipse (drag, solar pressure, gravity harmonics)

beginnerMistakes: Using simplified propagators for missions where perturbations matter
Not updating atmospheric density models for accurate drag predictions
Ignoring leap seconds and time system conversions
